QF.id: XIII Sales Practices, Code of Conduct and Investor Protection_10

question.text: What is the SMART ODR (Online Dispute Resolution) mechanism for resolving investor disputes?

answer.text: SMART ODR is a SEBI-mandated online platform for resolving investor disputes through conciliation (21 days) and arbitration (30 days) — making the process faster, paperless, and accessible from anywhere
SMART ODR is a physical arbitration center at the exchange premises
SMART ODR is a voluntary mediation service that has no binding authority
SMART ODR only handles disputes above Rs. 1 crore — smaller disputes go to consumer courts

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SMART ODR (launched by SEBI) enables online resolution of investor disputes. Process: (1) Conciliation attempt within 21 days — a neutral conciliator tries to help parties reach agreement, (2) If conciliation fails, arbitration within 30 days — an arbitrator makes a binding award. For claims above Rs. 30 lakh, a panel of 3 arbitrators is constituted. **The Trap:** SMART ODR is online (not physical), has binding authority (arbitration awards are enforceable), and handles disputes of all sizes (no minimum threshold). **Cross-Exam Context:** SMART ODR applies across all exchange-traded segments including derivatives. Series I, IV, and VIII Ch10 all reference the dispute resolution mechanism. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** SMART ODR significantly reduces resolution time and cost compared to traditional courts. Investors should use this mechanism before escalating to SEBI or SAT.
